steps.title: Identify the moisture-void-oil relationship
steps.content: The passage explicitly traces a causal chain: water evaporates from the crust → creates voids → oil (a pure fat) fills those voids. The clear implication is that more water loss means more voids, and therefore more oil infiltration.

steps.title: Apply the relationship to sections losing less moisture
steps.content: Sections that lose less moisture undergo less water evaporation, which creates fewer voids for oil to fill. With fewer voids, less oil can infiltrate into those sections, producing a lower final fat content. The other choices address crust thickness, permeability rate, and temperature—none of which follow directly from the moisture-void-oil pathway the passage describes. That matches choice B — B is correct.
